zh.Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United: 美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農
zh.lifeSummary: 當Benoni Root Clark於1730年9月7日出生在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農時，他的父親Benoni Clark37歲，母親Hannah Root31歲。他於1750年11月12日與Ruth Carpenter在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆勒班農結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他於1761年住在美國康乃狄克州。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Benoni Root Clark于1730年9月7日出生在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農时，他的父亲Benoni Clark37岁，母亲Hannah Root31岁。他于1750年11月12日与Ruth Carpenter在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆勒班農结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他于1761年住在美国康涅狄格州。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Benoni
meanings: Hebrew: Son of grief or trouble

nameMeanings.name: Root
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern informal short form of names such as Rupert and Ruth . In A. A. Milne's Winnie the Pooh, Roo is the name of the irrepressible child of Kanga, whose name therefore represents the second element of the word kangaroo.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: British Forces Capture Fort Griswold
historicEvents.description: The capture of Fort Griswold was the final act of treason that Benedict Arnold committed. This would be a British victory. On the American side 85 were killed, 35 wounded and paroled, 28 taken prisoner, 13 escaped, and 1 twelve year old was captured and released.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of the Battle of Groton and The Burning of New London

historicEvents.description: Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: Brass is Discovered
historicEvents.description: "In 1802, brass was identified in Waterbury, Connecticut. This gave the city the nickname ""The Brass City."" Brass dominated the city and helped to create the city. The motto of the city is Quid Aere Perennius, which means What is more lasting than brass? in Latin."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Connecticut History.Org- Birth of the Brass Valley

historicEvents.title: Financial Relief for Public Land
historicEvents.description: A United States law to provide financial relief for the purchasers of Public Lands. It permitted the earlier buyers, that couldn't pay completely for the land, to return the land back to the government. This granted them a credit towards the debt they had on land. Congress, also, extended credit to buyer for eight more years. Still while being in economic panic and the shortage of currency made by citizens, the government hoped that with the time extension, the economy would improve.

historicEvents.title: Farmington Canal Opened
historicEvents.description: Farmington Canal spans 2,476 acres, starting from New Haven, Connecticut, and on to Northampton, Massachusetts. The groundbreaking for the canal was in 1825 and opened in 1829.
historicEvents.urlTitle: ConnecticutHistory.org- New England's Grand Ambition:The Farmington Canal

en.lifeSummary: When Benoni Root Clark was born on 7 September 1730, in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United States, his father, Benoni Clark, was 37 and his mother, Hannah Root, was 31. He married Ruth Carpenter on 12 November 1750, in Lebanon, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He lived in Connecticut, United States in 1761.

ja.lifeSummary: Benoni Root Clarkが1730年9月7日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンで生まれた時，父Benoni Clarkは37歳，母Hannah Rootは31歳であった。1750年11月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ウィンドハムレバノンでRuth Carpenterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1761年にはアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州に住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: Benoni Root Clark(이)가 1730년 9월 7일 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benoni Clark(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Hannah Root(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1750년 11월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 윈드함 레바넌에서 Ruth Carpenter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 1761년도에 미국 코네티컷 주에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Benoni Root Clark nasceu em 7 de setembro de 1730, em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Benoni Clark, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Hannah Root, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Ruth Carpenter em 12 de novembro de 1750, em Lebanon, Windham, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele viveu em Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América em 1761.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Benoni Root Clark родился 7 сентября 1730 г. в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Benoni Clark было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Root, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Ruth Carpenter 12 ноября 1750 г. в местности лебанон, виндхам, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он жил в местности Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в 1761 году.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Benoni Root Clark nació el 7 de septiembre de 1730, en Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, su papá, Benoni Clark, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Hannah Root, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Ruth Carpenter el 12 de noviembre de 1750, en Lebanon, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Vivió en Connecticut, Estados Unidos en 1761.
